deep_dives.thesis: MU is a strong outperformer (+124% 3M vs SPY +113%) riding the AI memory cycle, with forward P/E of 6.44 implying the market prices in a near-tripling of earnings from trailing levels. The uptrend is intact with price 4% above 50MA, but the magnitude of the rally and extreme forward earnings expectations create asymmetric downside if memory pricing or AI capex cycles falter.
deep_dives.moat_assessment: MU benefits from high switching costs in DRAM/NAND memory design-ins and oligopolistic industry structure (SK Hynix, Samsung, Micron), but moat is cyclical and capital-intensive rather than franchise-like, making it vulnerable to supply gluts and capex cycles.
deep_dives.macro_sensitivity: Highly sensitive to AI capex cycle (NVDA/MSFT/GOOGL spending), memory spot pricing, and USD strength (Korean Won/JPY competition); moderate rate sensitivity via capex financing costs; VIX spikes would hit high-beta semis disproportionately.
key_catalysts: Next earnings date: 2026-09-24 — guidance revision will be critical given forward P/E 6.44 expectations
Memory spot price trends (DRAM/NAND) and AI accelerator build-out pace from hyperscalers
deep_dives.disconfirming_evidence: A miss on forward guidance or memory price softening would collapse the 6.44 forward P/E multiple rapidly; trailing P/E 21.69 offers no margin of safety if earnings growth decelerates.
deep_dives.bull_scenario: AI capex cycle accelerates, memory prices firm, MU guides above consensus — +20-25% toward $1,200-1,250 as forward P/E holds.
deep_dives.base_scenario: Consolidation between $950-1,050 as market digests 124% rally; uptrend intact but momentum fades — flat to +5% over 3-21 days.
deep_dives.bear_scenario: Memory price correction or AI capex pause triggers de-rating of forward multiple — -15-20% toward $800-850 as trailing P/E 21.69 compresses.

deep_dives.thesis: NVDA is in a short-term downtrend (0.3% below 50MA at $209.40) despite the bullish sector regime, suggesting the AI capex narrative is being digested after a 14% 3M rally. Forward P/E of 16.22 vs trailing 32.47 implies the market expects earnings to roughly double, yet revenue growth is only 0.852% — meaning the growth thesis depends entirely on margin expansion and mix shift rather than top-line acceleration.
deep_dives.moat_assessment: NVDA's CUDA ecosystem creates high switching costs and platform lock-in for AI training workloads, but the moat is increasingly contested by custom silicon (Google TPU, Amazon Trainium, AMD MI300X) as hyperscalers seek to reduce dependency on a single supplier.
deep_dives.macro_sensitivity: Extremely high beta to VIX spikes and AI capex cycle sentiment; moderate rate sensitivity via growth-stock discount rate; USD strength impacts international revenue but less than domestic demand drivers.
key_catalysts: Next earnings date: 2026-08-27 — guidance revision will be critical given forward P/E 16.22 expectations

deep_dives.disconfirming_evidence: An earnings miss or conservative forward guidance would rapidly compress the forward P/E 16.22 multiple, especially given the low 0.852% revenue growth rate — there is no top-line cushion if margins plateau.
deep_dives.bull_scenario: Earnings beat with raised guidance and no material custom silicon share loss — +20-25% toward $250-260 as forward P/E holds.

deep_dives.thesis: INTC has rallied 119% over 3M despite negative profit margins (-5.90%) and no trailing P/E, making this a pure turnaround bet priced at a forward P/E of 61.68. The stock is in a downtrend, 13.5% below its 50MA at $115.80, with volume rising +9% on the decline — a bearish technical setup that suggests the rally is losing steam. With earnings due today (2026-07-24), this is a binary event where the premium is being paid for a recovery that has not yet shown up in the financials.
deep_dives.moat_assessment: INTC's x86 architecture and integrated manufacturing once created high switching costs, but the moat has eroded as customers migrated to ARM-based designs and TSMC-led foundry competition; any recovery depends on regaining process leadership and foundry relevance.
deep_dives.macro_sensitivity: High beta to semiconductor cycle and AI capex trends; moderate rate sensitivity via growth-stock discount rate; USD strength impacts international competitiveness against TSMC/Samsung.

deep_dives.moat_assessment: META's network effects across Facebook, Instagram, and WhatsApp create powerful scale advantages in digital advertising, but the moat faces structural risk from TikTok competition, ATT/privacy headwinds, and Reality Labs capex dilution.
deep_dives.macro_sensitivity: Moderate rate sensitivity via growth-stock discount rate; USD strength impacts international ad revenue translation; VIX spikes would hit but less than high-beta semis given 32.84% profit margin cushion.

deep_dives.thesis: NEE is in a sideways consolidation (+2.4% above 50MA at $87.70) with volume collapsing -75%, suggesting the market is frozen ahead of earnings TODAY (2026-07-24). The 3M underperformance (-2.56% vs SPY +10.92%) reflects rate sensitivity and renewable energy headwinds, but the forward P/E of 20.40 on 7.3% revenue growth and 29.37% profit margin is reasonable for a regulated utility. With earnings today, this is a binary event where the collapsed vol suggests a muted post-earnings reaction unless guidance surprises materially.
deep_dives.moat_assessment: NEE benefits from massive scale in regulated utility operations and a leading renewable energy development pipeline, but the moat is increasingly contested by rising interest rates that increase financing costs for capital-intensive renewable projects.
deep_dives.macro_sensitivity: Extremely high rate sensitivity due to capital-intensive renewable project financing costs; moderate USD strength impacts minimal (domestic utility); low VIX sensitivity as a defensive sector; benefits directly from rate cuts and suffers from rate hikes.
